title: Slow-cooker coq au vin
subtitle: Chicken thighs braised in red wine with lardons and mushrooms
excerpt: Chicken thighs browned and braised with lardons, mushrooms, red wine, and thyme for six to eight hours. The wine softens to a rich, glossy sauce around the chicken. Serve with mashed potato or crusty bread to absorb the sauce.
sourceNotes: Coq au vin is a Burgundian peasant dish that gained popularity in Britain through Julia Child's Mastering the Art of French Cooking (1961) and the rise of French bistro cooking. The slow cooker version follows the same method as the original (browning, fond, wine deglaze, long braise) but removes the need to monitor the pot on the stove for 90 minutes.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the mushrooms if possible — they become soft on reheating. Add browned mushrooms fresh.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. Refrigerate in the sauce and skim the fat before reheating.

content.text: Do not skip the browning step. The fond (the dark residue in the pan after browning the chicken and frying the lardons) is the foundation of the sauce's depth. Deglaze it completely with wine before adding it to the slow cooker. Without it, the result tastes of stewed chicken rather than braised.

Fry the {{lardons}} in a wide pan over medium heat until the fat renders. Remove and add to the slow cooker. Increase the heat to high. Season the {{chicken-thigh}} with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}} and brown skin-side down for 4 minutes without moving, then flip for 2 minutes. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{onion}} to the pan and fry for 2 minutes. Add the {{garlic}} and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the {{red-wine}} and scrape up all the browned bits. Add the {{stock-chicken}} and bring to a simmer. Pour over the chicken. Tuck in the {{thyme-fresh}} and {{bay-leaves}}. Cook on low for 6 to 8 hours.

In the last 30 minutes, fry the {{mushrooms-chestnut}} in the {{unsalted-butter}} over high heat until browned. Stir into the slow cooker. Remove the {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs and {{bay-leaves}} before serving.

items.symptom: Sauce thin and wine-forward rather than rounded
items.cause: Wine not reduced before the slow cooker, or not enough fond
items.fix: Always bring the wine to a boil and deglaze the pan thoroughly. Raw wine added directly to the slow cooker stays sharp over the cook time

items.cause: Added raw rather than browned first
items.fix: Always fry mushrooms in butter over high heat before adding. Raw mushrooms release too much water in the slow cooker and lose all texture

items.cause: Slow cooker produces steam rather than dry heat
items.fix: Remove the thighs 10 minutes before serving and place under a hot grill, skin-side up, for 5 minutes. The skin crisps quickly under direct heat

content.text: Coq au vin is a weekend dinner rather than a weeknight dish — the prep requires 30 minutes of active work. The slow cooker then handles the braise without any attention for six to eight hours, producing a result that would otherwise need 90 minutes of stovetop supervision. Serve on mashed potato with the sauce spooned generously over.
